parts.section_subtitle: A phone enquiry about joining a community centre.
passage_paragraphs: Receptionist: Good morning, Maplewood Community Centre. Caller: Hi, I'd like to become a member. Receptionist: Of course — can I take your name? Caller: It's Sarah Bennett. Receptionist: And your address? Caller: I'm at flat 4, Brookside House. Receptionist: Could you spell that? Caller: B-R-O-O-K-S-I-D-E. The postcode is GL7 2RD. Receptionist: And a phone number? Caller: Yes, it's 07700 916482.

Receptionist: What type of membership would you like? Caller: A family membership, please — it's for me and my two children. Receptionist: And which activity interests you most? Caller: Mainly the swimming classes. Receptionist: Those run on several evenings; which suits you? Caller: Tuesday evening would be ideal. Receptionist: The family rate is £32 a month.

Caller: Is there any discount available? Receptionist: We offer a discount for students, though the family rate is already reduced. Receptionist: How did you hear about us? Caller: I picked up a leaflet at the library. Receptionist: Lovely. When you come in, please bring proof of address, such as a recent utility bill.

question_groups.instructions_html: Complete the form below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

layout.title: MAPLEWOOD COMMUNITY CENTRE — New member form

items.html: Heard about the centre from a ___

parts.section_subtitle: A talk introducing Riverside Park to new visitors.
passage_paragraphs: Welcome to Riverside Park. Let me point out the main facilities. As you come through the entrance, the café is the first building you reach, right where the path meets the lake. The children's playground is a little further on, beside the river bend just below the café. If you'd like to get out on the water, boat hire is up at the top of the lake.
For quieter visitors, the bird hide sits at the far eastern end, across the river. And the information centre is right at the top of the main path, over on the far side near the trees.

A few things to know. The park is busiest on Saturdays, so come early at the weekend. This year we've added a new wildflower meadow near the south gate. Throughout the park, dogs must be kept on a lead. Our free guided walk leaves the café at 11 a.m. every day. And if you'd like to volunteer, the easiest way is to fill in a form online.

parts.section_subtitle: Two students, Maria and Tom, plan a river-study project.
passage_paragraphs: Maria: So, let's divide up the river project. Tom: I'll handle collecting the water samples, since I've already got the kit. Maria: Great — I'll book the minibus, then. Tom: The introduction we should probably write together. Maria: Agreed, both of us on the introduction. Maria: I can make the graphs once we have the numbers. Tom: And I'll check all the references at the end.

Maria: Let's agree the stages. First we choose a sampling site on the river. Then we collect data over two weeks. After that we enter the results into a spreadsheet. We look for patterns in the figures, and finally we write the report.

question_groups.instructions_html: Who will be responsible for each task? Choose the correct letter, A, B or C, for each task.
Complete the flow-chart below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.

steps.html: Choose a ___ site on the river

parts.section_subtitle: A short lecture on urban rooftop gardens.
passage_paragraphs: Today I'll talk about urban rooftop gardens. The idea first became popular in the city of Berlin, and has since spread worldwide. Their main benefit is that they lower a building's temperature, which cuts cooling costs in summer.
There are two broad types. Extensive gardens are light and use mainly succulent plants that need very little care. Intensive gardens are more like ordinary gardens and need much more maintenance.

Construction follows a clear sequence. A waterproof membrane is laid down first to protect the roof. Next comes a layer for drainage, so that water doesn't pool. Then a lightweight substrate replaces ordinary soil, because normal earth is far too heavy.

There are challenges too. The extra weight placed on the roof has to be checked by an engineer. The higher initial cost can put owners off. And in hot climates, the need for regular watering in summer adds to the workload.

question_groups.instructions_html: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
